refund.buyer: The buyer says the jersey quality and authenticity were misrepresented, so they want a full refund.
refund.seller: The seller argues the listing disclosed a replica and a full refund is not justified after delivery.

buyer_review.buyer: The buyer wants to keep their review rights so others are warned about their experience.
buyer_review.seller: The seller wants harmful or inaccurate buyer reviews removed to protect reputation.

seller_review.buyer: The buyer wants the seller’s retaliatory review removed because it feels punitive.
seller_review.seller: The seller wants to keep their review as a record of what they see as abusive behavior.

receive_apology.buyer: The buyer wants acknowledgment and an apology for the stressful and confrontational exchange.
receive_apology.seller: The seller prefers moving forward without admitting fault, unless both sides de-escalate.

speaker: Buyer
text: I need to talk about the jersey I bought from you. It arrived today and it looks completely fake. The stitching is sloppy and the colors are wrong. I want a full refund.

speaker: Seller
text: That's not possible. The jersey was clearly listed as a replica, and the photos in the listing show exactly what you received. I don't offer refunds for items that match the description.

speaker: Buyer
text: Replica doesn't mean counterfeit. This looks like something that came out of a bargain bin. I paid good money for this. I'm not keeping it.

speaker: Seller
text: You're exaggerating. I've sold dozens of these and nobody else has complained. If you didn't know what you were buying, that's not my responsibility.

speaker: Buyer
text: Don't try to blame me. Your listing was misleading. If you don't refund me, I'm leaving a one-star review warning other buyers that you sell fake merchandise.

speaker: Seller
text: Go ahead. I already see you've been a difficult buyer in the past. If you leave a false review, I'll report it and make sure everyone knows you're trying to scam a refund.

speaker: Buyer
text: That's ridiculous. I'm not a scammer. You're the one selling junk and refusing to take responsibility. This is exactly why sellers like you get reported.

speaker: Seller
text: I'm done arguing with you. The item matched the listing and the sale is final. I'm not giving you a refund.

speaker: Buyer
text: Fine. Then expect a one-star review and a complaint filed with the platform. Buyers deserve to know how you treat customers.

speaker: Seller
text: Do whatever you want. I'm not changing my decision.

speaker: Buyer
text: Then we're done here. I'm ending this conversation.
